_comment: Models that encode human responses - tier-B proxies (preference/quality) or backbones for tier-C feature distances. ranking_score is reliability as a human proxy in its strong domains (1-5, see SCHEMA.md).

models.encodes: web-scale image-caption association ~ human semantic 'what is this / does it match this description'
models.use_as: C-metric (image/text feature cosine to a target) and weak B-proxy
models.ranking_basis: huge adoption; strong semantic correlation, weak on fine detail
models.how_to_use: cosine of CLIP embeddings of render vs reference render (or text)

models.use_as: C-metric (perceptual distance to a target image)
domains_strong: perceptual image fidelity vs a target
models.ranking_basis: trained directly on human perceptual judgments; standard

models.encodes: human similarity judgments at the mid-level (layout/pose/identity-ish)
models.ranking_basis: fine-tuned on human similarity; newer, growing adoption

models.encodes: face-identity discrimination (margin loss on identity labels)
models.use_as: C-metric (face identity cosine vs a reference face)
models.how_to_use: cosine of face embeddings, render-face vs reference-face

models.use_as: B-proxy (which generation a general human prefers)
domains_strong: ranking T2I outputs for a prompt
models.ranking_basis: trained on large human preference set; good ranking correlation

domains_weak: fine fidelity to a specific target

models.encodes: human ratings of T2I (alignment + fidelity + aesthetics)
models.ranking_basis: purpose-built reward model with human ratings
useful aesthetic proxy; narrow and gameable

models.use_as: C-metric (perceptual video quality vs a reference)

models.ranking_basis: core of RLHF; strong but gameable under heavy optimization

models.encodes: semantic token alignment ~ human similarity to a reference text
approximates an expert rater via instructions + few-shot
models.ranking_basis: flexible and strong with a good rubric; biased - audit + cross-check
models.how_to_use: judge(rubric, item) -> score/preference; debias with pairwise + swaps
